Hey what's going everyone? Welcome to Lead Nurture Principles. Understanding these two tips we will be with getting leads in the door. So let's get to it. So the lead nurture process is a combination of speed and volume. So one of our core tenants at Gym Launch in general still is Speed is King and that's been carried over since my days at the gym. Speed is King. Immediately. So you already have your Zapier SMS setup so that your phone should be tap to call. And you should do that twice. So you should be doing a double dial. Make sure that you always follow FTC guidelines at the time of this making it's 8am to 9pm in terms of call hours just to make sure that you don't cross any lines you're not supposed to. So tap to call that will decrease how much you work. And so if you can stay on it then you will have to call way less and spend less time calling and more time selling. The other piece of this is that the faster time they schedule and the time that you have reached out to them the dramatic increase in likelihood that they will actually show up for the sale. So speed increases the proximity to their pain and increases the proximity to their interest. So the further out you are so if you opt in and I call you within 5 minutes you will be tomorrow and I only call once from a number I don't know like the likelihood that you get me you know you can see how the numbers dramatically go down. But be faster. Second piece is volume is king and the reason I didn't say queen is because they are twins they are twin pillars to making this work and making a lot of money. This is going to be your competitive advantage. The reason I have workload realities is the second point here is that like this will be the work. This is the work that will separate whether you will succeed or not. I am just being very candid. The more reach outs you have the number one predictor of how much money you will make is the number of reach outs you have. So if you want to decrease the amount of reach outs you have call faster but still the number one driver once we have looked at all these campaigns is directly proportional with how many. So just a quick you see the second main set of bullet points is volume and money math. Ten dollars per call is what we have figured out so we have tracked calls over hundreds of campaigns when we were running these on our own and that is the math. So for every 50 calls there is going to be one sale. Mind you you can do 50 calls in an hour or you can take you all day right but the question is would you rather make more money or less. So ten dollars per call so if you feel like you are like man I don't feel like doing this just think about getting paid a ten dollar bill every single time you dial the phone over the same thing for your employee. So if you had set the goal to make 50 thousand dollars for a launch that would mean 100 sales that would mean you should have roughly 400 leads that means you are closing 25% of your sales which is what you are doing awesome. So if you have 100 sales and you sell 500 that is 50 thousand over 30 day sales cycle that is 14 leads a day and following our ten dollar per call is 167 calls a day if you have a one month long campaign. That is what it boils down to 167 calls a day period. So that means you can just call for four hours a day and call 40 calls per hour for four and hit it. So if you do that for a month you will make the money that you want to make. Primary principles are the primary drivers of success with this process. I mean it. Volume and speed.
